Package org.apache.juneau.bean.jsonapi

package org.apache.juneau.bean.jsonapi
Juneau @Marshalled types for the JSON:API v1.1 wire format.
Overview

JSON:API is a media-type (application/vnd.api+json) for building APIs with shared conventions for resources, relationships, links, errors, and metadata. This module provides typed Juneau beans modeling the JSON:API wire format - no marshaller is needed; the existing JsonSerializer / JsonParser already produce and consume the wire format. The matching ContentType constant is org.apache.juneau.http.header.ContentType#APPLICATION_VND_API_JSON.

Bean Classes
  • JsonApiDocument - Top-level container with data/errors/meta/jsonapi/links/included.
  • JsonApiResource - Resource object with type/id/attributes/relationships/links/meta.
  • JsonApiResourceIdentifier - Slim form used inside relationship linkage.
  • JsonApiRelationship - Relationship object (data linkage, links, meta).
  • JsonApiError - Error object with id/links/status/code/title/detail/source/meta.
  • JsonApiErrorSource - The source sub-object (pointer/parameter/header).
  • JsonApiLink - Link object with href/rel/describedby/title/type/hreflang/meta.
  • JsonApiVersion - The jsonapi member (version/meta).
  • JsonApiLinkOrStringSwap - ObjectSwap for links map values; each value can be either a JSON string URL or a JsonApiLink object per the spec.
Important: JsonApiResource.type is a plain String

JSON:API uses type as an open-ended string field on resource objects (the entity-type name, e.g. "articles"); the value is chosen by the API and is not a closed Java class hierarchy. Therefore JsonApiResource models type as a plain String and is not annotated with @Marshalled(typePropertyName="type", dictionary=...). Conflating JSON:API resource typing with Juneau bean dispatch would break round-tripping of unknown type values and force every API to enumerate its resource types in a Java dictionary up front.

Document-level mutual exclusion

Per the JSON:API spec, a document MUST contain at least one of data, errors, or meta; data and errors MUST NOT coexist. JsonApiDocument documents this constraint and offers an optional validate() helper.

Example

// Build a compound document with included resources. JsonApiResource article = new JsonApiResource() .setType("articles").setId("1") .putAttribute("title", "JSON:API paints my bikeshed!"); JsonApiDocument doc = new JsonApiDocument().setData(article); String json = JsonSerializer.DEFAULT.serialize(doc);

See Also:

  • Related Packages
    Package
    Description
    org.apache.juneau.bean
    Data Transfer Objects
  • Classes
    Class
    Description
    JsonApiDocument
    Represents the top-level JSON:API document as defined by JSON:API v1.1 § Document Structure.
    JsonApiError
    Represents a JSON:API Error Object as defined by JSON:API v1.1 § Error Objects.
    JsonApiErrorSource
    Represents the source sub-object of a JSON:API Error Object.
    JsonApiLink
    Represents a JSON:API Link Object as defined by JSON:API v1.1 § Links.
    JsonApiLinkOrStringSwap
    Per-property ObjectSwap applied to JSON:API links maps so that each map entry round-trips as either a JSON string URL or a JsonApiLink JSON object per the spec.
    JsonApiRelationship
    Represents a JSON:API Relationship Object as defined by JSON:API v1.1 § Relationships.
    JsonApiResource
    Represents a JSON:API Resource Object as defined by JSON:API v1.1 § Resource Objects.
    JsonApiResourceIdentifier
    Represents a JSON:API Resource Identifier Object as defined by JSON:API v1.1 § Resource Identifier Objects.
    JsonApiVersion
    Represents the JSON:API top-level jsonapi member.
